# PPI Scout v0.5.0

This is the first public release of PPI Scout, a local-first workflow for
planning, running, and conservatively interpreting reproducible Boltz
protein-complex and motif-peptide screens.

## Windows: start here

Download only
[`PPI-Scout-Windows-Installer-v0.5.0.zip`](https://github.com/ZHULUYI-KyushuU/ppi-scout/releases/download/v0.5.0/PPI-Scout-Windows-Installer-v0.5.0.zip),
extract the ZIP, and double-click `Download-Install-and-Run.cmd`.

The first setup needs an internet connection and downloads approximately
10.4 GB. It verifies every downloaded part with SHA-256 before installation.
After setup is complete, structure-prediction runs can operate without an
internet connection.

Requirements:

- 64-bit Windows 10 or Windows 11
- WSL2 enabled
- at least 40 GB of free disk space recommended
- a Windows NVIDIA driver with WSL2 support for CUDA acceleration

The release also contains the individual verified parts used by the installer.
Most users should not download those parts manually.

## Simplified first-release interface

PPI Scout exposes four main commands:

- `ppi-scout doctor` checks the installation and accelerator.
- `ppi-scout plan` verifies inputs and writes a reviewable job file.
- `ppi-scout scan` lists AIM/LIR sequence candidates.
- `ppi-scout run` prepares or executes a reviewed job and writes all reports.

`ppi-scout run` automatically creates matched motif controls, resumes unfinished
work, and writes a confidence table, a Markdown report, and a self-contained
local HTML result page.

## Scientific notice

A predicted structure or confidence score is not experimental evidence of
binding. Compare wild type and matched controls under identical settings, and
validate important findings experimentally.
